name: Scenario.Simple

unions.name: Config
unions.comment:
Configurations for the `view`.
  * `title`: Main title of this chat
  * `buttonLabel`: Label name of submit button
  * `namespace`: A name space for css.

unions.name: Model
unions.comment: A `Model` for this module. Combine this with parent `Model` in the manner of the elm architecture.

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: A `Msg` for this module. Combine this with parent `Msg` in the manner of the elm architecture.

values.name: config
values.comment:
A constructor for `Config` type.
  * `title`: Main title of this chat
  * `buttonLabel`: Label name of submit button
  * `namespace`: A name space for css.
values.type: { title : String, buttonLabel : String, namespace : Namespace String CssClasses id Msg } -> Config id

values.name: css
values.comment:
A CSS component.
Please reffer to the `rtfeldman/elm-css` for detail.
Also, you can see an example in `sample/simple/`.
values.type: Namespace String class id msg -> Stylesheet

values.name: init
values.comment:
Create a initial `(Model, Cmd Msg)` for this module. By providing a scenario,
you determine which schenario should be used by default. To construct a `Scenario`,
please reffer to the `print`, `read`, `andThen`, `andAlways` in `Scenario` module.

values.name: update
values.comment: A `update` for this module. Combine this with parent `update` function in the manner of the elm architecture.

values.name: view
values.comment:
A `view` for this module. Combine this with parent `view` function in the manner of the elm architecture.
The first argument is supposed to be constructed by `config` function.

name: Scenario

unions.name: Scenario
unions.comment: Main type of this module to represent scenario.

values.name: andAlways
values.comment: Similar to `andThen`, but ignores previous state.
values.type: Scenario c t v b -> Scenario c t v a -> Scenario c t v b

values.name: andThen
values.comment: Combine two scenarios to make one scenario.
values.type: (a -> Scenario c t v b) -> Scenario c t v a -> Scenario c t v b

values.name: config
values.type: (t -> Cmd msg) -> Cmd msg -> (c -> Cmd msg) -> Config msg c t v

values.name: map
values.comment: Convert scenario state by given function.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Scenario c t v a -> Scenario c t v b

values.name: print
values.comment: Construct scenario contains only one print message event.

values.name: pushAnswer
values.comment: Push answer to a scenario and get next scenario
values.type: v -> Scenario c t v a -> Scenario c t v a

values.name: read
values.comment: Construct scenario contains only one read input event.
values.type: c -> Scenario c t v v

values.name: succeed
values.type: a -> Scenario c t v a

values.name: update
values.type: Config msg c t v -> Scenario c t v a -> ( Scenario c t v a, Cmd msg )
